title: rosa rubiginosa subsp. agrestis
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subsp. agrestis, also known as wild dog rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subsp. agrestis include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb for various health concerns. Rosa rubiginosa subsp. agrestis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is also used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
